We visited here toward the end of a long day which started about 0400 in Ohio so didn't have much energy for a long hike. Even so we found the park to be a beautiful place.  We were especially pleased with all the natural trees and plants - tulip trees, Doug firs, apples, mountain ash, blueberries (the latter two not definitely identified but they looked like those types from our experience in the East.) There are well maintained trails all over and as all the guide books promise - good views of Puget Sound.  Also found the remnants of Ft Lawton - with handsome officers quarters now privately owned and the base chapel. Reminiscent of Ft Worden on the peninsula where Officer and a Gentleman was filmed. We think it would be nice to have some maps and direction signs here and there and perhaps a few park benches. Maybe  they were there but we didn't venture far enough to see them.

I had a difficult time enjoying this spot with the water treatment plant in the middle of the park.  It might have nice views of the sound but there are lots of spots around Seattle with great views of the sound that do not have a water treatment plant nearby.
The day we visited, it was Sunny and 72*.  We hiked to the Lighthouse and down to the Beach. We had a Picnic looking out on the water and enjoying the sound of the waves.
